tasks.system: You are writing a 3-minute internal video — the Monday-morning Treasury review walkthrough. Output in markdown with screen-recording cues + VO. Chase's voice. Operator-direct.
tasks.prompt:
Write a **3-minute screen-recording walkthrough** of the weekly Monday-morning Treasury review. Chase records this once, Tracy + the accountant follow the same checklist every Monday going forward.

Format: 16:9, screen-record of `/admin/treasury` + commentary, ~450 words VO.

Arc:
1. Open Treasury, glance at top three cards: MRR, churn, bucket hours. What's a healthy number for each.
2. MRR by tenant — week-over-week, look for surprises (sub up = good; sub down = investigate)
3. Tier 3 engagements — which are active, which are new this week, any churned
4. Bucket hours — current month consumption per consuming entity. Are we tracking to under or over the bucket?
5. Per-entity P&L — pick one entity per week to deep-dive (rotate)
6. Any red flags — past_due subscriptions, dead-letter events, incident postmortems pending
7. End-of-review action — text Chase any anomalies, otherwise the review is done

Markdown. ~1300 words including click cues.

tasks.system: You are writing the internal team communication norms doc. Output in markdown. Direct, no corporate-policy boilerplate.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **MBT team communication norms** doc — for everyone on the team (Chase, Tracy, Amy, Studio C team, future hires) so we don't burn time on coordination overhead.

Cover:

1. **The channels** — what to use when:
   - Text (iMessage) — time-sensitive, day-of issues, quick yes/no
   - Slack — async ops, team-wide visibility, links/files
   - Email — external partners, documentation trail, anything HR/legal
   - Asana — project tracking, anything multi-week
   - Phone call — strategic, conflict, anything high-stakes

2. **Response time expectations**:
   - Text → 1 hour during waking hours
   - Slack → same business day
   - Email → 1 business day
   - Asana update → next standup

3. **Standup cadence** — weekly Monday 9am CT, async-friendly via shared doc

4. **Escalation rules** — when does a peer-level question become a 'text Chase' question? Concrete heuristic: if a customer is currently impacted, escalate now.

5. **Decision-making norms** — disagreement-then-commit principle. Equity partners get vetoes on partner-level decisions. Operational decisions stay with whoever owns the area.

6. **Documentation norms** — if you decide something that affects others, write it down (Asana note, doc edit, Slack pin). 'It's in my head' = it's lost.

7. **Permission norms** — equity partners trust each other to act in good faith without pre-approval. Default = ship the small thing, ask first on the big thing.

8. **Disagreement norms** — say it directly, in the channel, with the data. No back-channel grumbling.

9. **Out-of-office norms** — let the team know 24+ hours in advance. Don't expect responses while on vacation.

10. **What NOT to do** — long Slack threads (move to a doc), middle-of-night texts (use scheduled send), surprise calendar invites without context

Markdown. Honest, specific. ~2000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing the internal team weekly rhythm doc — what gets done when. Output in markdown. Direct.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **MBT weekly rituals** doc — the recurring rhythm of the team week.

Format: day-by-day. Each day lists who does what, with check-in/output cadence.

Monday morning:
- 9am CT standup (Chase + Tracy + Amy)
- Treasury review (Chase + Tracy walk through dashboard)
- Magazine editorial planning (Tracy)
- Radio episode planning (Amy)
- Studio C team standup (Elijah + Miles + others)
- Active customer review (Vicki + future tenants)

Monday afternoon - Friday:
- Daily ops as appropriate
- Tracy: Inn day-to-day + 1 magazine article via wizard + 1 Sanity publish
- Amy: Radio production + bar at Inn + Records artist outreach
- Chase: pitch meetings + strategic + code/deploys + photography commissions

Friday afternoon:
- Recap + metrics check
- Tracy: weekly performance report draft
- Amy: weekly content + engagement summary
- Chase: pipeline review + any partner outreach

Monthly:
- First Monday of month: full team review (3 hr block)
- 15th: invoice + payroll close
- Last Friday: monthly customer review calls (Vicki, etc.)

Quarterly:
- Q-end strategy session (Chase + Tracy + Amy, full day)
- Customer renewal conversations
- Pricing review

Annually:
- Year-end retreat
- Cap table review
- Insurance renewal

Voice: direct, prescriptive. Markdown. ~2000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing the monthly board update template — for the three equity partners' monthly partnership review. Output in markdown.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **monthly equity-partner update template** — Chase fills this out the first Monday of every month for Tracy + Amy.

Sections:

1. **Headline number for the month** — single most-important metric (e.g., 'MRR up 12% MoM' or 'closed first broker-tier client')
2. **MRR + cohort snapshot** — total MRR, by tenant, by brand. Net new + churned.

7. **Customer losses of the month** — names + reasons

Voice: direct, no corporate-fluff, peer-to-peer with equity partners. Markdown template ~1800 words including all placeholders + brief explanation of each section.

tasks.system: You are writing a DRAFT refund policy. Output in markdown. Plain English. NOTE: Mark clearly at top as DRAFT for legal review.
tasks.prompt:
Write a **DRAFT refund + cancellation policy** covering all MBT subscription products + one-off purchases.

MUST start with: '> **DRAFT — REQUIRES LEGAL REVIEW BEFORE PUBLICATION.**'

Cover:

**Subscriptions** (DSD, Records label services, Tuthill realtor packages, Radio sponsor packages):
- Cancel anytime, no contract
- Cancel-at-period-end is the default (customer keeps access until billing period ends)
- No refund for the current period (industry standard, except where noted below)
- Notable exception: documented service failure on our side gets a pro-rated refund

**One-off orders** (merch, prints, books, tickets):
- Tickets: refundable up to 24 hours before show, after that no refund (transferrable instead)
- Physical merch: 14-day return window if unopened
- Digital downloads: no refund (industry standard)

**Inn bookings** (direct):
- Free cancellation up to 7 days before stay
- 50% refund 3-7 days before
- No refund within 3 days, except for documented emergencies
- OTA bookings follow OTA policy

**Quote-to-deposit private events**:
- Deposit refundable up to 30 days before event
- 50% within 14-30 days
- Non-refundable within 14 days

**How to request a refund**:
- Self-serve via Stripe Customer Portal (subscriptions)
- Email billing@ for everything else
- 5 business day processing time

**Disputes**:
- Contact us first before chargeback
- Stripe dispute process if unresolved

Plain English. Markdown. ~2000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing an internal prompt engineering guide for the team. Output in markdown. Concrete, with examples.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **MBT prompt engineering guide** — for anyone on the team who interacts with AI tools (the Wizard, manual Gemini batches, Claude Code agents).

Cover:

1. **The voice firewall** — every brand has a voice profile in `docs/voice/`. Always reference it. Never let an AI pick voice freely.
2. **The Humanizer rule** — every AI text output runs through the forbidden-phrases check. List of universally forbidden phrases (extends per-brand): leverage, synergy, unlock, elevate, transform, journey, cutting-edge, seamless, unparalleled, etc.
3. **Concrete > vague topics** — 'the Blues Room shows in May, Tracy Lake Trio Friday' beats 'music coverage'.
4. **Entity grounding** — always pull real entities from the directory. Never let the AI invent businesses or musicians.
5. **Photo grounding** — always pull real assets from Immich. Never let the AI 'imagine' a photo description.
6. **Length targets** — every content type has a target. Force the AI to respect it.
7. **Few-shot examples** — when output drifts, attach 2 example drafts that match the desired voice.
8. **The Gemini batch dispatcher** — `scripts/ai/gemini-batch.mjs` — when to use, batch size norms, output path conventions, voice + format enforcement in system prompts.
9. **The Wizard** — when to use vs manual Gemini batch (Wizard for Sanity-published content, batch for everything else).
10. **Common prompt failures + fixes** — generic AI-blog tone (force forbidden-phrases list); fictional entities (force entity-grounded prompt); wrong length (force min/max); wrong voice (specify brand profile).

Markdown with example prompts. ~2500 words.

tasks.system: You are writing a complete inventory of services running on the Mac mini. Output in markdown. Reference-grade.
tasks.prompt:
Write the **Mac mini services inventory** — every service running on `192.168.4.37` (ClawdBOT user), what each does, ports, credentials, restart procedure.

Services to cover:

1. **OpenBroadcaster** — port 8080, http://192.168.4.37:8080, admin/bigmuddy2026, used for radio scheduling
2. **Icecast** — port 8010, http://192.168.4.37:8010, streaming server for Big Muddy Radio
3. **Plex** — port 32400, http://192.168.4.37:32400, media server
4. **Postiz** — port 4007, http://192.168.4.37:4007, social media scheduler
5. **Open Notebook** — port 5055, http://192.168.4.37:5055, knowledge management
6. **OBS Studio** — local app + WebSocket plugin on port 4455 (TBD), used by broadcast agent for TikTok Live scene control
7. **broadcast-agent** (NEW — Phase C Block 4) — Node service polling MBT server every 30s for OBS / ffmpeg / clip-upload instructions, runs as launchd agent

For each service:
- What it does (1 sentence)
- Port + URL
- Credentials location (Bitwarden item name)
- How to restart (concrete command)
- How to check it's healthy (concrete check)
- Logs location
- Common failures + fixes

Also include:
- SSH access pattern: `ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_mini ClawdBOT@192.168.4.37`
- Backup strategy for each service's data
- Boot-time persistence (launchd plists for each)
- Network requirements (must be on LAN with the MacBook for some workflows; must have internet for cron-driven services)

Markdown. ~2500 words.
